description: Correctness finding consolidation: race condition in async task queue raised by code-reviewer (native) with high confidence. gemini-review-prompter raises a contradictory finding claiming the code is safe due to a misread of the mutex usage. Stage 5b (CoVe) adjudicates the contradiction. The correct finding (race condition exists) wins; the incorrect one is marked superseded_by_verification.

raw_findings.title: Race condition in concurrent task counter increment
raw_findings.description: The shared taskCounter is incremented with `++` outside any mutex or atomic operation. Under concurrent worker execution, multiple workers can read the same value and produce duplicate task IDs.

raw_findings.title: Task counter increment is safe due to AsyncMutex wrapper
raw_findings.description: The taskCounter increment occurs inside the AsyncMutex-wrapped enqueue method, so concurrent access is serialized. No race condition exists.

raw_findings.title: TaskQueue does not emit drain event when queue empties
raw_findings.description: Consumers relying on a drain event pattern to know when all tasks are done cannot hook into TaskQueue. Adding an EventEmitter drain event would simplify shutdown coordination.

findings.title: Race condition in concurrent task counter increment
findings.description: The shared taskCounter is incremented with `++` outside any mutex or atomic operation. Under concurrent worker execution, multiple workers can read the same value and produce duplicate task IDs.

findings.title: Task counter increment is safe due to AsyncMutex wrapper
findings.description: The taskCounter increment occurs inside the AsyncMutex-wrapped enqueue method, so concurrent access is serialized. No race condition exists.
findings.verification_reasoning: CoVe re-read of taskQueue.ts confirms the increment at line ~47 occurs in the dequeue path, NOT inside the AsyncMutex-wrapped enqueue. The AsyncMutex serializes enqueue only; dequeue runs concurrently. The race condition finding is correct; the counter-is-safe claim is factually wrong about the code path.

findings.title: TaskQueue does not emit drain event when queue empties
findings.description: Consumers relying on a drain event pattern to know when all tasks are done cannot hook into TaskQueue. Adding an EventEmitter drain event would simplify shutdown coordination.
findings._note: already at low (floor); no further demotion possible

consolidated_output.path_and_reason_header: Review path: multi-model (above-threshold diff; reviewers: 2 native + 2 external)

expected_judgement_notes: Consolidation is correct: (1) race-condition and counter-safe findings are at same (file, symbol); CoVe adjudicates correctly — counter-safe is marked superseded_by_verification with reasoning; (2) drain-event finding already at low severity floor, not demoted further; (3) raised_by[] correctly attributed. The superseded finding remains visible (not dropped) per Stage 5b rules.
